findings.summary: A generic utils facade adds indirection despite already having focused helper modules
evidence: `src/shared/lib/utils.ts` only re-exports symbols from narrower modules (`uiClassNames` and `stringFormatting`) instead of adding behavior.

`src/shared/components/StyledVideoPlayer.tsx` imports `cn` via `@/shared/lib/utils`, while `src/shared/components/VideoPortionTimeline/index.tsx` imports `formatTime` directly from `timeFormatting`, showing mixed abstraction entry points.
findings.suggestion: Pick one boundary strategy for shared helpers: either remove `src/shared/lib/utils.ts` and import from focused modules directly, or create an intentional single facade (including time helpers) and migrate consumers to it. Add an ESLint import rule to enforce the chosen path.
findings.reasoning: Inconsistent helper entry points make ownership and discoverability weaker, and the extra pass-through layer provides little leverage while increasing indirection.

findings.summary: Sibling video components apply very different decomposition strategies
evidence: `src/shared/components/StyledVideoPlayer.tsx` combines playback control, mute/fullscreen logic, event wiring, loading/error handling, and full controls UI in one large component.

`src/shared/components/VideoPortionTimeline/index.tsx` is primarily orchestration and delegates behavior to hooks/components (`usePlayhead`, `useHandleDrag`, `SelectionOverlay`), indicating a more modular abstraction style.
findings.suggestion: Refactor `StyledVideoPlayer` into a composition root plus extracted units: a playback/controller hook (event listeners, constrained playback, time state), a controls overlay component, and a loading/error overlay component. Keep shared formatting (`formatTime`) at the boundary only.
findings.reasoning: When similar UI areas use mismatched abstraction depth, maintenance and reuse become uneven; the monolithic path is harder to test and evolve than the delegated pattern already used nearby.
